title: Level 1
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 1: "Parking Zone"}} {{CSS|content=@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Aleo:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&display=swap')}} {{CSS|content=@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Wavefont:wght@100&display=swap')}} {{CSS|content=@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Protest+Revolution&display=swap')}} __NOTOC__{{Class 1|color-h=0|color-s=0%|color-l=50%|hexagon=1|class=1|safety=Safe|security=Stable|entity=Minimal Entity Count}} '''Level 1''' is the second level of the Backrooms, and one of the only levels wanderers can enter directly through [[the Frontrooms]].
'''General Description'''
 {{Underline|FFFFFF}} {{Blurimage|caption=The most iconic image of '''Level 1'''.|file=Level_1.jpg}} '''Level 1''' resembles a typical parking garage, with concrete pillars, hanging fluorescent lights, and stairwells present at regular intervals. Though it is a parking lot, the number of cars is minimal, with roughly five cars in any 100-square-kilometer plot at the level at a time. Cars on the level have no brand or license plates and are typically red, blue, white, or black. All cars are faulty, but the alloy they are made of, a mixture of carbon, iron, platinum, and titanium, is a valuable metal which can be collected for weaponry or armor. This alloy is named tripse and is often traded for other items held in high regard. Oftentimes, puddles on the floor composed of '''[[Object 1|Almond Water]]''' will evaporate and condense into a thick fog, reducing visibility for wanderers who traverse through these parts; curiously, these puddles never seem to fully evaporate, instead lingering permanently. '''Level 1''' has a consistent and reliable source of electricity for both the lights and various wall outlets, which help many groups survive. Due to the ease of reaching this level, many wanderers opt to either join or form a group to ensure their survival. However, this level contains various dangers that wanderers must be wary of. Examples include blackouts which occur within this level and last from minutes to even days, leaving the level without light. During such blackouts, entire sections of the level will be plunged into complete darkness, allowing entities to emerge and hunt more successfully. When traversing darker areas, it is advisable for wanderers to carry flashlights to deter unwanted encounters. Additionally, pieces of rebar or sharp metal may occasionally jut out from walls, harming any wanderers who are not careful. Because most of these metal pieces are coated in a thick layer of rust, wanderers will almost definitely contract tetanus if they pierce the skin. The room temperature at this level is quite high, averaging roughly 30 to 35 degrees Celsius (86 to 95 degrees Fahrenheit). However, it has been noted to drop significantly in foggy areas. This level is roughly the same size as '''[[Level 0]]''' and exhibits non-Euclidean properties that make traversal difficult at times. Occasionally, crates filled with supplies such as '''[[Object 1|Almond Water]]''', canned food, and weapons can be found scattered around, spawning and disappearing at irregular intervals, which can aid wanderers in their journey. However, there is a chance for these crates to be simply filled with '''[[Object 12|Liquid Pain]]''', though the reason for this is unknown. Lastly, there are several special areas within '''Level 1''' that are sufficiently different in appearance and properties to warrant specialized sections.
'''Unique Areas'''
 {{Underline|FFFFFF}} Below are the areas listed within '''Level 1'''. ==='''Maintenance Halls'''=== {{Blurimage|caption=An image of a maintenance hall.|file=Level 1 Hallway.jpg}} Maintenance halls can be accessed by a jet black, metallic door within the main section of '''Level 1'''. The walls of these hallways replace the usual grey concrete with white, which may seem bright to many wanderers due to the contrast with the main area. Typically, objects such as plywood, toolboxes, and even whole tables can be found littering the floor. Fewer entities tend to reside in these halls compared to the main area. These halls also usually contain items such as exposed wiring, faulty pipes leaking '''[[Object 1|Almond Water]]''', and power boxes. Although they have electricity coursing through them, these power boxes do not actually supply electricity to the rest of the level and instead seem to have no real purpose. Due to environmental hazards, it is not recommended to shelter in any of these halls for an extended period. However, wanderers may want to stay here briefly in the event of a blackout, as the power supplied to these hallways is unaffected by blackouts. ==='''Luxury Lots'''=== {{Blurimage|file=Level_1_Rewrite.jpg|caption=An image of a luxury lot.}} Large, empty spaces often filled with dense fog span for miles—taking up a sizeable section of '''Level 1'''. Pipes, electrical outlets and electrical boxes are all quite common to come across when traversing these luxury lots, yet it is to be noted that most of these either lack any electricity or are incredibly faulty—one should always be wary of that when operating such devices. However, even in cases where they are powered by electricity, the exact source powering these devices remains unknown, though it is presumed they generate their own power through alien means. When electricity courses through these outlets and boxes, however, the voltage is extremely high, reaching output levels of up to a gigawatt. How these devices handle such power levels is unknown. These '''luxury lots''' are inherently empty and can be accessed by simply ascending the stairs behind various doors on '''Level 1'''. The most notable difference compared to the main area of '''Level 1''' is the brighter color scheme of the walls and the enhanced cleanliness, particularly the lack of debris and dust on the floor. However, these lots are popular places for groups to set up base, and wanderers should exercise caution to ensure they do not encounter a hostile group. Although the '''Luxury Lots''' do indeed experience power outages as well, these are usually resolved within the hour, unlike the main section. Lastly, these '''Luxury Lots''' have the potential to be the nest of various entities; hence, wanderers must be cautious when exploring these areas. However, the main advantage of exploring these areas is the increased frequency of supply crates containing useful items and the fewer entities compared to the main section.
'''Entities'''
 {{Underline|FFFFFF}} Some entities which reside here are '''[[Entity 9|Facelings]]''', '''[[Entity 5|Clumps]]''', '''[[Entity 8|Hounds]]''', '''[[Entity 6|Dullers]]''' and '''[[Entity 4|Deathmoths]]'''. However, there are two level-exclusive entities which reside in '''Level 1'''. ==='''False Puddles'''=== {{Blurimage|file=False_Puddle.jpg|caption=A sketch of a False Puddle's anatomy.}} '''False Puddles''' are entities which disguise themselves as simply '''[[Object 1|Almond Water]]''' puddles; however, beneath the surface is a deep pit filled with sharp teeth. These entities are unable to move, and instead depend on unaware wanderers to fall into their trap. They have no true body and are essentially big mouths filled with teeth and '''[[Object 1|Almond Water]]'''. False puddles are typically 150 to 360 centimeters deep. Although they are hard to find, there are ways to eliminate them once found. One way is to pour '''[[Object 12|Liquid Pain]]''' into the puddle, which will cause corrosion, and they will close their mouths. Another way is to throw items such as sharp metal pieces into their mouths to trick them into snapping shut, and the metal may pierce their mouth lining and subsequently bleed them to death. However, finding such puddles is already a challenge in itself, given how many regular puddles are present within '''Level 1''', which require a lot of supplies to truly ensure a single area is safe. ==='''Paintings'''=== Poorly drawn paintings and other drawings occasionally line the walls of these warehouses, most commonly found in the long yet empty maintenance halls. Sometimes elegant canvas frames; other times, coarse pieces of paper hung on the wall with string and rough nails hammered into the brick wall. These '''paintings''' can be found not only on the walls but also, rarely, on the floor or the ceiling, frequently featuring ladders or ropes for access in the latter case. These paintings and drawings are to be avoided when possible, as, in actuality, most of these supposed paintings are living entities. This doesn't apply in all cases, but every drawing that shows organisms should be avoided if possible, as such drawings most likely pose a threat to one's own safety. These beings could possibly reach out from their respective illustrations and clutch any nearby wanderer, dragging them into the painting. What exactly happens afterward is unknown; however, it is assumed that these paintings share behavioral patterns with '''[[Entity 2|Predatory Windows]]''' and therefore act similarly. There's also a possibility of said wanderer being represented in the drawing itself, as several of them show humanoids in distress who, however, seem to be entirely unresponsive, unlike the typical predatory versions. While this isn't always the case, in most cases these drawings pose a great threat to wanderers unfamiliar with this environment, especially when in a rush or in darker areas, where one may not anticipate an attack. The paintings and drawings vary in their pictorial context; some may be crude drawings, while others may express prodigious portraits from seemingly hundreds of years ago. Despite this, the contents of each artwork do not correspond with anything found in '''[[The Frontrooms]]''', meaning that these entities—perhaps even '''Level 1''' itself—are likely to be the perpetrators behind the art itself, as it is entirely unique from actual portraits. Unnervingly, there have been rare accounts of the eyes in certain portraits moving, and even of the entity itself moving erratically on occasion.
'''Additional Information'''
 {{Underline|FFFFFF}} A seemingly nonsensical string of letters was recently found written on a wall with an unknown substance. The letters will be provided below, and the [[M.E.G.]] urges any wanderers who can decipher this code to tell us its meaning. KKWRHBQOHRLKZWWHVKKDQNHLVZOQOLHFWKDVIXWRHHKHVGHQVOOQVGDDGVLQSUPWDQLLQIWNLQHZHRGOHIJWRHUKHFWUDHXWDVWKHLUGVKZHLHODOYVXIHUVPKRHWHDVVZHDWQOVG Additionally, a note was found on the floor within a '''Maintenance Hall''', with the words being written in fading black marker.
Day 5 Still stuck in this hellhole. I've gotten out of those yellow rooms, but now I'm in a... warehouse? It's strange, to say the least. No one is here, it's so empty. Might walk around, explore a little. My marker is fading, so I'll be writing less from now on. Update: I found a crate, but it was full of this black, foul-smelling liquid. Yeah, I'm ''not'' touching this. Day 8 The lights just went out. I hear something in the dark, gonna try to take shelter in a hallway. This place is crazy; I saw some paintings, but... their eyes were tracking my movements. Oh, fuck. I saw something with glowing eyes in the dark. It had... five eyes? What? Whatever, this place doesn't make sense anyway. Day 9 Saw that guy again. Got a closer look and saw he was wearing some sort of suit, but it was tattered. Wonder if it's one of those creatures or just a guy in cosplay. Hmm... Day 11 That... ''thing'' with the five eyes saved me. Killed a dog-looking creature while it was sneaking up behind me in the dark. It looked at me, and just left. I have NO idea what that thing is, but I'm honestly grateful. Definitely not human, I can at least say that much. Day 12 I can't stop thinking about that thing. I saw so much of it when I turned my flashlight towards it. Had a nightmare last night because of that incident. I need to draw out what I remember, for the sake of anyone else in this place. Oh God, I finally found an exit. I think. Weird door that I've never seen before. Leaving this page here for someone else to find, hopefully it helps them.
'''Outposts, Bases and Colonies'''
 {{Underline|FFFFFF}} There are many groups and bases due to the safety of '''Level 1''' compared to most other levels, however only the major ones shall be listed down. ==='''[[Backroom Colonists]] Base Blink'''=== *Owned by the [[Backroom Colonists]]. *Friendly and open to trade. *Population ranging from 30 to 60, depending on the number of people stationed there at any given moment. *Often interacts with other friendly groups in the level. ==='''The Raiders'''=== *Recently discovered with a rough count of 78 members. *They are mostly friendly with regular wanderers and are willing to trade but will shoot down members of certain groups. *Often hoards supplies. *Promise to take down all the big groups within the Backrooms to get rid of capitalism. *Base has fallen, no survivors. ==='''Cultus Sancti'''=== *Comprises of roughly 50 members. *Eliminated The Raiders to "appease the holy one". *Sacrificial. *Hostile to all who are not in their cult. ==='''[[M.E.G.]] Base First Light'''=== *Has a member count of 60. *Guides lost wanderers to safer areas. *Open to trade.
'''Entrances and Exits'''
 {{Underline|FFFFFF}} A plethora of entrances and exits have been discovered, however only the ones which are the easiest to access will be listed. ==='''Entrances'''=== *Opening doors in many levels has a small chance of leading to the level. *Wanderers are able to enter this level from [[Level 0]] by walking up a flight of stairs. *Unstable flooring in [[Level 13]] indicated by visible, odd textures the floor possesses may lead wanderers to this level if they can no-clip through it. *People may occasionally no-clip to this level from [[the Frontrooms]] instead of [[Level 0]]. ==='''Exits'''=== *Wanderers can exit '''Level 1''' by simply continuing to explore, as it usually leads to [[Level 2]] or back to [[Level 0]] via staircases or hallways. *An unlocked door may lead to [[Level 2]]. *[[The Hub]] can be accessed from this level by walking up two flights of stairs and then immediately walking back down. Due to the non-Euclidean properties, wanderers will then be met with a split passage. Wanderers must take the left hallway, then proceed with the right one. After doing so, they will come across a door labelled "A" and one labelled "B". Enter the "B" door, then the "A" door on the other side. *No-clipping through walls that have outlets may lead to [[Level 188]]. ---- {{Collapse|num=2|initial='''> Open Author and Licensing Credits'''|final='''> Close Author and Licensing Credits'''|body=

P.s. if you actually took the time to open this, run the string of letters through a permutation cipher, then a Caesar cipher. Key for the perm cipher has something to do with the backrooms btw.}} {{Redirect|Prev=0|Next=2}} [[zh:Level 1]] [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:The Main Nine]] [[Category:Safe]] [[Category:Cluster I]]
